title: Phegopteris moussetii

This fern is further classified into the genus Phegopteris, a group of epiphytic ferns. The species Phegopteris moussetii is native to South America. intro: Phegopteris moussetii, also known as Mousset's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Phegopteris moussetii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phegopteris moussetii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and topical creams, with the latter being commonly used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
